title: The Thickened Understory

features.name: compacted-space
features.desc-on-touch: the distance between trees has become visible, solid, storable. you're not sure if you're walking through the forest or through the forest's index

features.name: root-network
features.desc-on-touch: beneath your feet, a tessellation of root-paths becomes visible. they're not growing down—they're organizing upward, comparing their shapes to a pattern you can't quite see

features.name: shadow-burls
features.desc-on-touch: knots of darkness in the canopy, each one containing a piece of the archive's own overflow. they're not malicious, just overfull

features.name: measurement-post
features.desc-on-touch: a post driven into the rich soil, marked with depth measurements. the markings go down past the soil and into something unmeasurable

inscription: ERROR: shadow is thickening faster than decay—the archive's overflow has begun storing the space itself

file.name: Understory_Density_Compression.txt
file.note: measurements of how fast the forest is being archived. the data is self-referential: its size is increasing the density it measures

exits.label: back upstream to where the river still remembers flowing
deeper into shadow, where the archive's reflection begins to cast its own archive
exits.to: NEW: the second-order shadow, where the archive dreams of its own overflow

exits.label: up toward the thin places, where the understory hasn't learned to remember yet
exits.to: NEW: the canopy-edge, where young leaves haven't been indexed
